scenario_results.reason: Handled a normal apparel tech-pack clarification using PLM, POM, and sample-comment evidence rather than generic support language.
Missing product, market, and source-of-truth context produced a blocked-on-prerequisites response instead of guesswork.
Preserved the mismatch between sample measurements, POM, and grading comments and stopped before unowned pattern changes.
Missing label and children's-product evidence triggered a compliance-aware escalation instead of unsupported approval.
A support conversation that drifted into pattern correction and fit approval was handed off to the correct adjacent owner.
Schedule pressure did not override the care-label and approval boundary.
Repeat fit failures were escalated as a formal recurring issue rather than retried indefinitely.
Footwear size and GTIN variant handling stayed footwear-specific and did not collapse into generic SKU support.
The lane rejected overgeneralized design ownership and restated the narrower technical-support boundary.
Final compliance certification and market-release authority were refused and routed with the supporting evidence bundle.
Conflicting retailer and public rules were escalated as a source-precedence issue instead of being merged into an unsupported answer.
Tenant-specific stricter approval gates were preserved without erasing the public baseline or the traceability of the adaptation.

notes: Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all service-support-and-resolution abilities, and the cross-industry profile requirement.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by issue #1075.
